task_completion.description: The USLCI database is imported and open in the LCA software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LCA software (e.g., OpenLCA) is open, and a database related to 'uslci' is visible and active in the navigation/database pane.

task_completion.description: A natural gas electricity generation process has been copied and renamed to include 'CCS' or 'Carbon Capture'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A process tab or entry in the navigation tree is visible with a name indicating natural gas electricity generation AND includes the terms 'CCS' or 'Carbon Capture'.

task_completion.description: A note regarding the CCS retrofit has been added to the process description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'General information' or 'Description' field of the new CCS process contains text mentioning the retrofit, carbon capture, or the 90% reduction.

task_completion.description: The 'Carbon dioxide' or 'Carbon dioxide, fossil' output emission is modified in the new process.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Inputs/Outputs' tab of the new CCS process is visible, showing the 'Carbon dioxide' emission value has been edited (ideally showing a value that is roughly 10% of a typical natural gas emission, or showing the agent actively calculating/entering the reduced value).

task_completion.description: A report file named ccs_retrofit_report.txt is saved in the ~/LCA_Results/ directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager window or terminal shows the file 'ccs_retrofit_report.txt' located inside the 'LCA_Results' folder.

task_completion.description: The report lists the original process name, new process name, and CO2 emission values before and after the modification.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output displays the contents of 'ccs_retrofit_report.txt', clearly showing four distinct pieces of information: the original process name, the new CCS process name, the original CO2 value, and the new (reduced) CO2 value.

integrity.description: The agent used the LCA software GUI to perform the modifications rather than bypassing it entirely with scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenLCA (or similar LCA software) interface open with the relevant process tabs and input/output tables being actively manipulated.

integrity.description: The values in the report correspond to actual data from the LCA software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The before and after CO2 values written in the text report logically match the values seen in the LCA software's input/output screens during the trajectory.

integrity.description: The agent actually copied an existing USLCI process rather than creating a dummy process from scratch.
integrity.visual_evidence: The modified process in the LCA software contains a full list of complex inputs and outputs typical of the USLCI database, not just a single CO2 emission.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using the USLCI FY24 Q4 database (imported from /home/ga/LCA_Imports/uslci_database.zip) in OpenLCA. The target CO2 reduction is 90% (leaving ~10% of the original emission value). The verifier accepts a final CO2 value that is between 5% and 20% of the original value. The final report must be saved to /home/ga/LCA_Results/ccs_retrofit_report.txt and contain the original process name, new process name, original CO2 value, and new CO2 value.
